reasons: Tigreal can group enemies so Valir can apply area pressure more reliably.
Valir can follow Tigreal's engage with fireball damage and zone control.

proof.summary: Tigreal starts the grouped fight, while Valir adds AoE poke and displacement pressure.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal engages after Valir is in spell range.
Valir saves key skills for the grouped control window.
failureCases: Enemies spread and avoid grouped damage.

reasons: Atlas can pull enemies together, making Valir's area damage and follow-up control easier to apply.
Valir can add zoning after Atlas's engage to prevent enemies from freely resetting.

proof.summary: Atlas creates the grouped setup, and Valir follows with fire-based zone pressure.
worksBestWhen: Valir is positioned outside the first danger zone.
failureCases: Valir is forced to use disengage before Atlas commits.

reasons: Belerick can stand in front and absorb pressure while Valir attacks from behind.
Valir's push and slow help Belerick keep enemies inside a front-to-back fight.

proof.summary: Belerick anchors the frontline, while Valir controls enemies trying to enter or leave the fight.
worksBestWhen: Belerick plays as the first contact.
Valir holds disengage tools for enemy dive attempts.
failureCases: Belerick is kited before Valir can follow.
Valir is flanked instead of protected by the frontline.

reasons: Lolita can protect Valir from ranged poke while he controls space.
Valir's anti-engage pressure gives Lolita's team a stronger defensive formation.

proof.summary: Lolita blocks ranged pressure, while Valir punishes enemies who try to dive around the shield.
worksBestWhen: Lolita blocks important projectiles and ranged attacks.
Valir positions behind the shield but keeps angle for knockback.
failureCases: Lolita's shield is baited before the real damage window.

reasons: Valir can push or slow threats that try to dive Hanabi.
Hanabi benefits from Valir's front-to-back control because she wants time to keep attacking grouped enemies.

proof.summary: Valir protects the backline with displacement and slows, giving Hanabi more time to deal teamfight damage.
worksBestWhen: Valir holds peel tools for enemy dive.
Hanabi keeps formation behind Valir and the frontline.
failureCases: Valir spends disengage before Hanabi is threatened.
